differential_diagnoses: Aortic dissection or symptomatic penetrating aortic ulcer
Spinal epidural abscess with associated radiculopathy
Septic pulmonary emboli from S. aureus endocarditis
Acute coronary syndrome secondary to septic emboli or stress
Osteomyelitis/Septic arthritis of the sternoclavicular joint or thoracic spine
next_steps: Urgent transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to evaluate for endocarditis and aortic pathology
Immediate vascular and cardiothoracic surgery consultation
Contrast-enhanced MRI of the thoracic spine to rule out epidural abscess
Repeat blood cultures to assess for persistent bacteremia
Maintain therapeutic antibiotic coverage (vancomycin and cefazolin) for S. aureus bacteremia
Strict blood pressure control to mitigate risk of aortic expansion/rupture
Avoid stress testing until aortic pathology is definitively excluded
